watched.name: Materialists
watched.review: I think the casting was my main/only real gripe with this movie. I love Song and her writing and while the dialogue didn’t always hit for me, I think that the underlying themes that she was trying to get across deeply resonated with me as they are the kinds of things I often think about as well. I think she takes a more pessimistic viewpoint on dating under capitalism but a more optimistic realism about love more generally. The relationship is not perfect and nor are the characters but that’s just real life!

watched.name: Thunderbolts*
watched.review: fun! but the third act kinda lost me

watched.name: A Pigeon Sat on a Branch Reflecting on Existence
watched.review: A film very rich in style; drab colors, fixed camera one take shots, and excellent set design and framing.The deadpan surrealist Nordic humor almost reads as whimsy but I would not call this a fun film.This movie wasn't really entertaining but it was still a good watch to just think about and analyze.

watched.name: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
watched.review: a good premise for heavier speculative fiction but instead tells a story about memory, love, and relationshipsi can’t say that i enjoyed my time with the characters themselves but it was interesting to see their actions/decisions bc it did at least feel realistic to me

watched.name: Warfare
watched.review: with this movie being focused on a realistic portrayal of just one military engagement the focus is not on the general war and interrogations on the justness of it (although civilian involvement is given a bit of thought while not the main focus) i think that the honest portrayal of war will resonate differently with everyone depending on their preconceptions it is gruesome and traumatic and messy but in a way that reads to me like it is not trying to glorify or fetishize it it was well produced with good cinematography and sound design and while not super artistic still had some good artistic choices with all that being said why this movie and why now? i think it is strange on garland and a24’s part to make and distribute this movie. maybe as a cautionary tale for war but it doesn’t seem as important as other stories in this moment

watched.name: I Love You, Man
watched.review: a really good entry into the rom-com canon bc of how it is about romantic and platonic love in a way that kind of mirrors each other narrativelyhas a lot of say on masculinity and male loneliness tbhaged fairly well for the most part and was progressive in some ways but still some iffy parts here and there

watched.name: Caught by the Tides
watched.review: a tough film to rate bc i was probably not the intended audience as someone who had never seen any of his other work — i was able to engage with it only abstractly it felt like there were at least two films in here in the three acts and i felt like the first act was my fav, but that’s not to say that the other acts didn’t have redeeming qualities  the editing and cinematography were beautiful but it did (understandably!) feel disjointed at times

watched.name: An Autumn Afternoon
watched.review: a story about children growing up and leaving the nest i can see why ozu is so influential, but i can’t help but feel like yang does it better (e.g. yi yi) because of how the characters have interiority and the dialogue is more interestingi think that ultimately this film, whether intentionally or not, is about the patriarchy which makes for an interesting watching experience, at least on the conceptual level i liked the cinematography but i personally could not stand how dialogue starts and ends within the same shot which could’ve just been a technical thing but it felt very noticeable overall i feel like this just didn’t connect much with me but i do respect it for what it is
